name: Black-Eyed Peas and Rice Salad
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces Frozen black-eyed peas (cooked according to package directions)
3/4 cup Extra-virgin olive oil (or part safflower oil)
1 Head of Radicchio or Boston lettuce for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. This salad version of the traditional Southern New Year's dish called Hoppin' John can be prepared a day ahead and stored in the refrigerator. Allow it to come to room temperature before serving.
2. Cook the rice and peas in advance.
3. Prepare the vinaigrette: Whisk together mustard, salt, pepper, and vinegar until dissolved.
4. Dribble in the oil while whisking.
5. Toss the black-eyed peas and rice with the vinaigrette until everything is nicely coated.
6. Mix in the onion, garlic, carrot, and chives or parsley.
7. Bring to room temperature before serving.
8. This dish can be prepared a day ahead and refrigerated, covered. Place in a bowl and surround with lettuce leaves; serve at room temperature.
